LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING BREACH OF CONTRACT AND BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT WAS AT-WILL, AND UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF SIGNED ACKNOWLEDGMENT STATING HE UNDERSTOOD HIS EMPLOYMENT AS AT-WILL. (UMF#8,9,10,13) ASSUMING PLAINTIFF CREATED A PRIMA FACIE CASE AS TO RACE, AGE, AND DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS, DEFENDANT ESTABLISHED A LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASON FOR TERMINATING PLAINTIFF. (UMF 29, 40) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ESTABLISH PRETEXTUAL REASON FOR THE TERMINATION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION OBJECTIONS SUSTAINED AS TO #4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 16, 21, 22, 25, 30, 32 AND 33. PORTIONS CONTRADICTORY TO PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION STRICKEN. (SEE D'AMICO VS. BD. OF MEDICAL EXAMINERS (1974) 11 CAL.3D 1, 21.) NO SPECIFIC AND SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATION. BUSSEY AND HOLMES DECLARATIONS LACK FOUNDATION AND NOT SPECIFIC. NO EVIDENCE OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY TO SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S RETALIATION CLAIM. (UMFS #76-78). NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT TO SUPPORT HARASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Plaintiff's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) si ordered off calendar and dissolved/good cause. Proceedings reported by: Helen Tamara Williat-Harrison, CSR#4609. (212)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Curtis G. Oler, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 346-8015, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Matt McFarland, Staff Attorney, Tenderloin Housing Clinic (415) 771-9850, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is granted. No triable issue of material fact regarding breach of contract and breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff's employment was at-will, and undisputed that plaintiff signed acknowledgment stating he understood his employment as at-will. (UMF#8,9,10,13) Assuming plaintiff created a prima facie case as to race, age, and disability discrimination claims, defendant established a legitimate business reason for terminating plaintiff. (UMF 29, 40) Plaintiff does not establish pretextual reason for the termination. Plaintiff's declaration objections sustained as to #4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 16, 21, 22, 25, 30, 32 and 33. Portions contradictory to plaintiff's deposition stricken. (See D'Amico vs. Bd. of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 21.) No Specific and substantial evidence of discrimination. Bussey and Holmes declarations lack foundation and not specific. No evidence of protected activity to support plaintiff's retaliation claim. (UMFS #76-78). No triable issue of fact to support harassment cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2009 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2009 Set For Trial On Aug-17-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-31-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 613
